title: Seniors centre leak fears
full_text:
The personal information of hundreds of north shore residents could have been leaked in a data breach involving Mosman Council.
An investigation is under way into how much detail has been accessed and how many people are affected by the apparent cyber attack.
Mosman Council has confirmed the information had been accessed through an external, or third-party, digital platform.
A council spokesman said the external platform provided software that was being used to help manage and deliver “some of the council’s community services”.
The Telegraph understands the data involved personal information related to the council’s Mosman Square Seniors Centre.
It posted a notice on its website on May 26, alerting locals about what it described as a “cyber incident”.
But because the incident related to a “third-party software system”, it did not affect other Mosman Council systems, which remained secure, the spokesman said.
The council declined to confirm which community services might have been affected.
It did say, however, that the breach appeared to form part of a broader cyber attack on the third-party provider, which had also affected a number of the third-party provider’s other customers.
The council confirmed on Tuesday it had brought in an external cybersecurity firm to work out what data had been accessed, the extent of the breach and “personal information it may contain”.
“Given that the incident occurred in the third-party provider’s environment, we are relying on information provided by the third-party provider,” the spokesman said. “The investigation is still currently ongoing.”
